statement.markdown: Given a number n, implement a function to check if it's prime. Note that the number is between 2 and 1,000,000,000. There might be a logical bug in the implementation.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a single integer n.
statement.outputFormat: Output is a boolean True if n is prime, False otherwise.

constraints.description: Input number to check for primality

hints.text: The current implementation has a logic error. Think about the properties of prime numbers.

editorial.title: Correct Primality Test Implementation
editorial.intuition: A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute-force approach can be used by checking for divisibility from 2 to sqrt(n). However, we need to optimize it further.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a boolean array to keep track of prime numbers up to sqrt(n).
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves generating a boolean array of size sqrt(n) and marking the multiples of each prime
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness proof involves showing that the algorithm produces the correct output for all prime and composite numbers.
editorial.dryRun: To dry run the solution, start with an example input and check the output.
commonMistakes: Not updating the boolean array correctly
